topic: A clearance application that needed three follow-ups, two visits, and four WhatsApp messages — for one client
format: Open with a specific Sanad or PRO failure moment — a visa that expired, a client who found out at the airport, a missed MOL filing — make it so specific the reader stops scrolling
post:
A client's clearance was rejected at the gate.

Not because the documents were wrong. Because the follow-up sat untouched for 11 days. Nobody flagged it. Nobody chased it. It just... sat there.

Three follow-ups. Two office visits. Four WhatsApp messages. All to fix one clearance that should have closed in 48 hours.

If you run a Sanad office, you already know how this happens.

The request came in on WhatsApp. It landed in a thread with 200 other messages. Someone was supposed to chase the ROP portal. Things got busy. The client assumed it was handled.

It wasn't.

Now multiply that by 20 client companies, 40 work permits, and 8 MOL filings this month. That's not a process problem. That's a capacity problem — and calling it a process problem is how offices keep absorbing the damage instead of fixing it.

SmartPro Hub was built for licensed Sanad offices in Oman. Structured case intake. Automatic expiry alerts. Task assignment per PRO officer. A client portal so companies can track their own cases without calling you every other day.

No more lost threads. No more clearances falling through on day three.

How many open cases in your office right now have no clear next action?
